description: The cross-tabbed feature surface (capability × tool) over the workbench/docs-tool landscape from backlog #624 (registry: src/_data/workbenchTools.json). Stage 2 of the Web Docs feature-pipeline epic #623: the union of capabilities a Web-Everything-native docs surface must cover or consciously omit. Each feature records which landscape tools ship it, how ubiquitous it is, a one-line 'what a WE-native version would need', and — the load-bearing column for the derivation decision #626 — its weStatus: whether it already maps to an EXISTING WE standard (reuse/extend), is genuinely NEW docs surface, or is OUT-OF-SCOPE for the standard layer. This pre-classification de-risks #626: roughly two thirds of the surface is reuse/extend of standards WE already has, leaving a small genuinely-new core (the isolation renderer, CEM-driven autodocs, long-form authoring, component search, source/code view).
refConvention: Tool ids reference workbenchTools.json sources; standard refs use project:<id> / block:<id> / intent:<id> / research:<id> / format:<id>.

ubiquityScale.label: Table stakes
ubiquityScale.test: Nearly every tool in its axis ships it; a WE-native surface without it would read as incomplete.

ubiquityScale.label: Common
ubiquityScale.test: Most leading tools ship it; expected but not universal.

ubiquityScale.label: Differentiator
ubiquityScale.test: A subset ships it and competes on it; optional but valued.

ubiquityScale.label: Niche
ubiquityScale.test: Few tools ship it; specialized or fading.

weStatusScale.label: Reuse existing standard
weStatusScale.test: An existing WE intent/block/project already covers this; the docs surface composes it, no new standard needed.

weStatusScale.label: Extend existing standard
weStatusScale.test: An existing WE standard is the right base but needs a docs-specific extension or composition recipe.

weStatusScale.label: Genuinely new surface
weStatusScale.test: No existing WE standard covers it; #626 must decide whether to mint a new intent/block/protocol.

weStatusScale.label: Out of scope for the standard
weStatusScale.test: A CI service, design-tool integration, or hosted product concern — belongs to tooling / the served product (#398), not the WE standard layer.

features.label: Component isolation rendering
features.description: Render a single component in isolation from its source/stories, decoupled from the app.
features.weNativeNeed: A renderer that mounts one WE block from its CEM/declarative definition in an isolated frame with no app shell.
features.note: Built on existing component/view blocks but the isolation renderer itself is the core new Web Docs surface. api-viewer proves a zero-build, CEM-driven web-component renderer is feasible.

features.label: Multiple states / stories per component
features.description: Enumerate and render multiple states/variants of a component (Storybook 'stories', Bit 'compositions').
features.weNativeNeed: A declarative 'examples' format binding a block to a set of named prop/state fixtures — ideally ingesting CSF and emitting its own.
features.note: Conceptually adjacent to webcases (test fixtures). #626 should decide whether stories are webcases reused for docs, or a sibling 'examples' concept.

features.label: Args / live controls
features.description: Live prop/attribute editing with typed controls (boolean toggle, enum select, color, text).
features.weNativeNeed: Derive control types from a block's CEM attribute/property types; render an editing panel — the Technical Configurator pattern aimed at one block.
features.note: The Technical Configurator (plateau-app) already data-drives a control panel from a schema; the docs 'args' panel is that pattern fed by CEM.

features.label: Source / code view + copy + sandbox
features.description: Show a component's source, copy-to-clipboard, and open an editable sandbox/embed (StackBlitz/CodeSandbox).
features.weNativeNeed: A syntax-highlighted code block + copy action + optional live sandbox embed for a block's example markup.
features.note: No WE code-display/syntax-highlight block exists yet; copy uses the data-transfer intent. The sandbox embed is an external integration (out-of-scope sub-part).

features.label: Autodocs from component metadata
features.description: Generated prop/slot/event tables from types, CEM, or react-docgen — no hand-authoring.
features.weNativeNeed: Emit CEM from WE blocks, then render it as API tables (api-viewer-style) — both the emit pipeline and the table surface.
features.note: The strongest #624 prior: CEM is the format to emit. This is the highest-leverage new surface — it makes WE blocks self-documenting and tool-ingestible.

features.label: Long-form docs (MDX/Markdown)
features.description: Markdown/MDX pages interleaving prose with live component examples.
features.weNativeNeed: A docs page format embedding live WE blocks in prose — Markdown with a block-embed directive, not framework-coupled MDX.
features.note: Authoring could reuse the rich-text-editing standard (#618/webediting) for the WYSIWYG path; the embed directive is the new bit. Starlight's near-zero-JS model is the native-first donor.

features.label: Viewport / responsive preview
features.description: Breakpoint switcher, device frames, responsive snapshots.
features.weNativeNeed: A viewport switcher wrapping the isolation frame, driven by the breakpoint intent's named breakpoints.
features.note: Fully covered by existing breakpoint standards; the docs surface just composes them around the frame.

features.label: Theme / dark-mode / token switching
features.description: Toggle dark mode, brand themes, or token sets and re-render components live.
features.weNativeNeed: A theme switcher bound to webtheme token sets, re-applying tokens to the isolation frame.
features.note: webtheme owns this; the switcher is a thin control over it.

features.label: Design-token tables
features.description: Render color/spacing/type scales as documentation tables from the token source.
features.weNativeNeed: Read webtheme tokens and render them via the existing data-table block — a composition, not a new standard.
features.note: A pure composition of two existing standards (webtheme + data-table).

features.label: Accessibility panel
features.description: Inline axe/contrast checks reporting a11y violations per component.
features.weNativeNeed: Run the webcompliance checks against the rendered block and surface violations in a panel.
features.note: webcompliance is the engine; the panel is a presentation of its output. Storybook v9 folded a11y into core — a signal it is now table-stakes-trending.

features.label: Interaction tests (play functions)
features.description: Scripted interactions + assertions run against a rendered component (Storybook play / Test).
features.weNativeNeed: Bind a fixture to an interaction script + assertions — overlaps webcases conformance fixtures.
features.note: Strong overlap with the exercise-app conformance loop / webcases. #626 should decide if docs interaction-tests are the same artifact as conformance cases.

features.label: Visual regression (snapshot/diff)
features.description: Snapshot rendered components and diff across changes, with hosted review/approval.
features.weNativeNeed: None at the standard layer — this is a CI service over the rendered output; the OSS path (Lost Pixel) could be a served-product tier.
features.note: A CI/hosted concern, not a WE standard. Belongs to the served product (#398) open-core hosted tier, mirroring Chromatic (paid) vs Lost Pixel (OSS).

features.label: Search across components & docs
features.description: Full-text search over component names, props, and long-form docs.
features.weNativeNeed: A static search index over blocks + docs with a search input (the input can reuse autocomplete/type-ahead; the index is new).
features.note: The input UI reuses existing blocks; the index/build step is the new surface. Starlight's Pagefind is the native-first donor (static, no SaaS).

features.label: Navigation / information architecture
features.description: Sidebar tree, tags, grouping, breadcrumbs — the catalog's IA.
features.weNativeNeed: A sidebar tree + breadcrumb over the catalog hierarchy — compose existing nav-list / tree-select / breadcrumb.
features.note: Fully covered by existing navigation standards (also the minimal first slice of #627: a /blocks/ index). Pattern Lab's Atomic Design taxonomy is the IA donor.

features.label: Versioning (multiple versions)
features.description: Browse multiple library/doc versions side by side; per-component versions.
features.weNativeNeed: Versioned doc snapshots + a version switcher; ties to the upgrader/migration standards for cross-version diffs.
features.note: Doc versioning is a new surface; cross-version migration content reuses the upgrader/webadapters work.

features.label: Docs internationalization
features.description: Localized docs content + locale switcher.
features.weNativeNeed: Localized doc routing + locale switcher — driven by webintl / locale + translation intents.
features.note: Covered by existing i18n standards.

features.label: Usage analytics
features.description: Track doc/component page views and engagement.
features.weNativeNeed: Page/component view tracking — reuse webanalytics.
features.note: A served-product concern that reuses webanalytics; relevant to the #398 hosted tier.

features.label: Access control / metered docs
features.description: Gated, personalized, or metered documentation (private design systems, paid tiers).
features.weNativeNeed: Gate doc routes/content by identity + entitlement — reuse access-control + web-identity.
features.note: Directly enables the #398 open-core tiering (free public catalog vs gated/per-customer docs).

features.label: Extensibility / addon model
features.description: Plugin/addon API to extend panels, toolbars, and rendering.
features.weNativeNeed: A registry-based extension seam for docs panels/tools — reuse the plugs/registries model rather than a bespoke addon API.
features.note: WE's plugs/registries are the native extension substrate; the docs surface is itself a registry consumer.

features.label: Design ↔ code bridge (Figma sync)
features.description: Sync component docs/tokens with a design tool (Figma) and compare design vs built.
features.weNativeNeed: None at the standard layer — a design-tool integration, not a web standard.
features.note: Benchmark-only. A possible future served-product integration, but not a WE standard. The fully-hosted version of this model (Backlight) already failed (#624).
